name: Nifty 50
summary: India joined global relief rally on April 1 (FY27 Day 1): Nifty ~22,800 (+2.1%) alongside S&P +2.9%, KOSPI +7.9%. But three-layer structural problem persists: crisis-reversible (~65%), structural (~25% — earnings, record borrowing ₹17.2T, bank NIM), regulatory (~10% — NOP cap $30-40B unwinding by April 10, STT hike). 200-day EMA at 22,000 = critical support (3.6% below). FPI record outflows ₹1.14L cr in March. India 10Y breached 7%. Even with ceasefire + $85 oil, only recovers ~65% of losses. FY26 ended worst in a generation (-13.9% from peak).

current.snapshot_note: As of Apr 1 intraday (Indian market open, March 31 was holiday)

price_snapshots.delta_context: Already down 5.2% YTD before full war impact

price_snapshots.trigger: Asia selloff on Trump power plant threats + FPI selling
price_snapshots.delta_context: Lowest since April 2025. ₹14T market cap wiped.

price_snapshots.trigger: Technical bounce from oversold levels, DII buying
price_snapshots.delta_context: Recovered +399 points (+1.8%). Still down 10.4% from one month ago.

price_snapshots.trigger: FY26's last trading day — worst close
price_snapshots.delta_context: Deepened from morning -0.9% to -2.14% as oil surged back to $115+ and rupee NOP rally reversed. MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%.

signals.content: Nifty futures dropped 1.7% on first trading day after strikes. Markets declined ~4% within two days. Goldman warned India 'most impacted in Asia.'
FIIs net sold ~₹52,704 crore in March. Single-day outflow ₹10,717 crore (largest of 2026). Societe Generale expects India underperformance to deepen. DII/SIP flows partially offsetting.

Nifty bounced +1.8% to 22,912 — technical recovery from April 2025 lows reached yesterday. DII buying provided support. But the bounce masks underlying weakness: FPIs sold ₹82,700 crore in March (largest monthly outflow since Oct 2024), India's PMI slowed to lowest since Oct 2022, and IPO market remains frozen. Total Indian investor losses since war began: ₹48 lakh crore (~$511 billion). The 'second only to Covid' framing in Indian media (Business Today) signals depth of concern.

Nifty fell 2.1% to 22,820 on Houthi war entry day. Sensex -2.2% to 73,583. FPIs pulled Rs 88,180 crore ($11.5B) in March. Critical support at 22,500 (200-day EMA). Bernstein warns Nifty below 20,000 if conflict persists through 2026.

Monday open: -0.9% to 22,612 despite rupee rally. Market seeing through mechanical NOP unwinding — oil at $107 still elevated, FPI selling continues. Nifty decoupled from rupee in this regime: both need OIL to fall, not just regulatory currency fix.

EVENING UPDATE: Nifty crashed from morning's 22,612 (-0.9%) to close at 22,331.40 (-2.14%, -488 points). Sensex settled at 71,947.55 (-2.22%, -1,635 points). This was FY26's LAST TRADING DAY — fiscal year ended on its worst note. Broader markets worse: MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%. Worst sectors: PSU Banks, Bank Nifty, Financial Services. Top losers: Bajaj Finance, Shriram Finance, SBI. FPI outflows hit record ₹1.13-1.14 lakh crore in March. The oil reversal ($107→$115) and rupee collapse (93.56→95.22) during Indian trading hours drove the deepening selloff. India entered FY26 as the world's fastest-growing major economy; it ended the fiscal year with record FPI outflows, record-low rupee, and Nifty -13.9% from peak. (Sources: Business Standard, Liquide, 5paisa, NSDL)

Nifty closed at 22,331 (-2.1%) — lowest since April 2025. FY26 ended as worst fiscal year since COVID. FPI outflows ₹1.13-1.14 lakh crore in March (RECORD). MidCap -2.68%, SmallCap -2.66%. Bank Nifty -3.8% (worst sector) on NOP cap blowback. FY26 ended -13.9% from peak. Split: ~70% crisis-driven (reversible), ~30% structural (earnings slowdown). Key level: 22,000 (200-day EMA). GIFT Nifty recovered from -1.84% to +0.16% for April 1 opening. Bernstein four scenarios: 27,500 (rapid de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis). CONFIRMED.

FY27 opened with gap-up (~22,550 from March 30 close of 22,331) on Pezeshkian peace signal; trading near 22,800 (+2.1%), joining the global relief rally (S&P +2.9%, KOSPI +7.9%). March 31 was an Indian market holiday. Three-layer structural problem persists regardless of rally: (1) Crisis-reversible ~65%: oil still above $100, FPI record outflows ₹1.14 lakh crore in March, rupee volatile around 93.5-94.3. (2) Structural ~25%: earnings slowdown, record FY27 borrowing ₹17.2 trillion, HDFC Bank NIM compression. (3) Regulatory ~10%: NOP cap forcing $30-40B unwinding (deadline April 10), STT on futures raised 0.02%→0.05%, options 0.10%→0.15%. NOP cap drove heavy bank selling on March 30 (Union Bank -6.55%, Canara -5.21%, BoB -5.07%). India 10Y breached 7% (first since July 2024). 200-day EMA at 22,000 — 3.6% below at current levels. Even if war ends and oil drops to $85, Nifty recovers only ~65% of losses. (Sources: 5paisa, Business Standard, Bloomberg, NSDL, TradingEconomics, yfinance verified)

edges.context: Primary equity benchmark. FY26 ended worst since COVID. India diverging from global rally. FPI record outflows. Bernstein scenarios range 27,500 (rapid de-escalation) to 19,900 (full-year crisis).
Weak rupee → FIIs sell → Nifty falls → more rupee pressure. Negative feedback loop. Rupee volatile 93.5-94.3 on NOP unwinding.

Oil spike → India macro deterioration → earnings downgrades → Nifty weakness. ICRA warned crude could 'derail' FY27 fiscal deficit target. ~65% of Nifty decline is crisis-driven (oil-dependent, reversible).
Markets need rate cuts for valuation support. RBI trapped by inflation — can't cut. NOP cap deadline April 10. Bank Nifty -3.8%, PSU banks hammered. India 10Y breached 7%.

trigger_points.mechanism: Psychological level. Would trigger more panic selling, margin calls, and potential capital controls discussion.

200-day EMA break triggers systematic/algo selling. Margin calls cascade. FPI selling accelerates. Currently 1.5% above at 22,331. This is the technical line that separates correction from bear market territory.
